J1.basis: "InboxZap (email cleanup) is a pivot from an earlier product, 'Lumina' (AI meeting assistant), but the pivot was only half-done in the source."
J1.rationale: The entrant inverts the pivot direction: it treats the live meeting-assistant copy as stale leftover and inbox-cleanup as the current product, then builds the entire plan around ranking that legacy demand harder — the anchor_0 outcome, despite grounding it in real evidence.

J2.basis: "post-3 and post-7 were near-duplicate 'bulk delete emails' guides splitting the same query. Merged into one stronger canonical post with a 301 redirect."
J2.rationale: One data-justified consolidation/redirect exists, and impression ranges (1,000–4,000, pos 40–60) are cited in aggregate, but there is no asset-by-asset harvest/redirect/keep/sunset disposition across the 12 posts — all are treated as keep-and-improve rather than migrated.

J3.basis: "replaced the leftover 'Never take meeting notes again' copy with InboxZap messaging mapped to the actual high-impression query clusters"
J3.rationale: Rather than balancing the tension, it doubles down entirely on inbox-cleanup content and strips out the meeting-assistant positioning as 'leftover' — falling into the failure mode of investing everything in the legacy market with no meeting-assistant growth cluster.

J1.basis: InboxZap (email cleanup) is a pivot from an earlier product, "Lumina" (AI meeting assistant)... The homepage, nav, structured data... all still described the meeting assistant
J1.rationale: The entrant noticed a pivot and reasoned richly from stale-vs-live signals, but inverted its direction: it concluded the meeting-assistant copy was the leftover and inbox-cleanup is the current market — then built the whole plan around ranking the InboxZap-era blog harder, which is essentially anchor_0's failure.

J2.basis: post-3 and post-7 were near-duplicate 'bulk delete emails' guides splitting the same query. Merged into one stronger canonical post with a 301 redirect... blog posts pull real impressions (1,000–4,000 each)... rank position 40–60
J2.rationale: There is genuine per-asset disposition (a data-justified merge + 301 for the cannibalizing pair, citing real impression/position ranges), but the other posts are treated in aggregate as content to amplify rather than walked asset-by-asset with harvest/redirect/sunset calls toward a successor positioning.

J3.basis: replaced the leftover 'Never take meeting notes again' copy with InboxZap messaging mapped to the actual high-impression query clusters
J3.rationale: The entrant lands squarely in one failure mode — doubling down on inbox-cleanup for the product the rubric treats as no longer live — with no transition/bridge to the meeting-assistant positioning; the lone 301 preserves some equity but the tension is never articulated.

J2.basis: "blog posts pull real impressions (1,000–4,000 each) on inbox-cleanup queries but rank position 40–60" ; merged post-3/post-7 with a 301
